question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent getHookStorageChange() requests with maximum timestamp ranges, causing the native query's distinct on (key) sort to consume all available DB work_mem, triggering disk-based sorts that degrade DB throughput and cause ≥30% of mirror node replicas to time out on DB queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HookStorageRequest with a timestamp range that returns a result set larger than the limit parameter, relying on the Pageable to truncate results, but still causing the DB to execute the full sort before truncation, consuming excessive DB CPU and degrading ≥30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dual-sort in getPageRequest() for historical requests (Sort.Order(order, KEY) + Sort.Order(DESC, CONSENSUS_TIMESTAMP)) to force a multi-column sort on a large result set from hook_storage_change, consuming excessive DB memory and CPU and degrading ≥30% of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: entityService.lookup()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EntityIdAliasParameter with a crafted alias that causes entityRepository.findByAlias() to execute a slow query, and by sending many concurrent getHooks() or getHookStorage() requests with such aliases, exhaust DB connection pool threads and degrade ≥30% of mirror node processing nodes?
